message:
feat(watchdog): tick 166 get_mux_server_rss_with_cx + check_cx routing (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Seventeenth post-storage tick. watchdog.rs has `check_cx` (cx-first
sibling of `check`, using the file's `_cx` naming convention
rather than `_with_cx`), but its inner `get_mux_server_rss().await`
call at L621 routed through the legacy helper — yet another
half-migrated cx-first entry.

New sibling (using `_with_cx` suffix per codebase convention, not
the file-local `_cx` variant):
- get_mux_server_rss_with_cx — pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()` gates
  spawning the blocking pgrep/ps fan-out. On a cancelled caller
  cx the function returns `None` (same sentinel as the legacy
  "process not found" path), so `check_cx` sees `rss_bytes = None`
  and skips the memory-threshold status check. The spawn_blocking
  body is still std::process::Command — cx isn't threaded inside
  the sync code — but the pre-flight means a cancelled
  watchdog.check sweep bails before triggering another pgrep
  shell-out.

Call-site migration in `check_cx` (L600):
- Line 621: `get_mux_server_rss().await` →
  `get_mux_server_rss_with_cx(cx).await`

Legacy `check` (L581) + `check_inner` (the non-asupersync path at
L643) preserved unchanged — both still route through the ambient
`get_mux_server_rss`.

caut.rs was a false positive this tick — it uses `_cx` suffix
throughout (`usage_cx`, `refresh_cx`, `run_with_cx`) so the scan
flagged the legacy names as "missing" even though their cx-first
siblings exist. event_stream.rs and watchdog.rs share this
convention. The updated scan filter accepts both `_with_cx` and
`_cx` as sibling suffixes.

Verified via fork bypass: 224/224 watchdog:: +
semantic_anomaly_watchdog:: tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
